nodes.name: Greg Mitchell
nodes.description: Stable at work, managing a team. Works long hours. Relationship with Drew is built on a shared language of competence and follow-through. Attended one of Drew's tournaments — sat in the bleachers, didn't cheer loudly, but was there. That mattered. On Thursday evening, Drew came to him about Sofia. Greg received it without flinching — engaged Drew as a young man rather than a child, gave advice, expressed quiet pride. He also registered, without saying so, that the window where Drew comes to him is finite and it's open right now. He has begun asking about the tech projects — the message board, the Alcatel phone — using that as a secondary bridge to deepen contact.

nodes.name: Karen Mitchell
nodes.description: Real estate career thriving. Megan is now 17 and the primary source of parental anxiety (driving, boys, college applications). Drew has been the easy child. That narrative is now complicated: she learned about Sofia on Thursday evening, after Greg, and the sting of being told second was real. She didn't express anger — that's not her register — but Drew saw the small anxious recalibration. Greg smoothed it. The easy child is becoming more complicated, and Karen's monitoring bandwidth, currently occupied by Megan, will begin to extend toward Drew in low-level, ambient ways. She drove Drew to the concert Friday evening in a car ride of controlled airspace — questions that weren't quite the questions she wanted to ask. When she dropped him off, 'Have a good time, Drew' landed with ambiguous weight. She is doing the math at the margins: who is this girl, who are her parents, what is her son becoming that she isn't fully seeing?

nodes.name: Megan Mitchell
nodes.description: 17, senior at Plano Senior High. College applications consuming the household. The age gap and temperamental difference mean Drew and Megan exist in parallel more than they interact. But Drew has noticed that Megan's boyfriend drama — the fights, the tears, the door-slamming — gives him a preview of what emotional volatility looks like when it's not managed. He's studying it without knowing he's studying it.

nodes.name: Carol Novak
nodes.description: Still the most important relationship in Drew's inner life. The Saturday visits have never stopped. Carol met Marcus and approved — quietly, the way she does everything. She gives Drew books that are always slightly above his level and trusts him to grow into them. She's the only person who gets the unfiltered version — and now that includes his emotional life, not just his thinking. She knows about Sofia: the letter, the courtyard lunches, the wanting. She received it without making it smaller. She is the second person outside Drew's peer world who holds this, and she holds it with characteristic care. She would not insert herself into the Drew-Sofia dynamic unless asked, but she is now a resource for it. The gardening detail — Drew telling Elena Reyes he wanted to learn skills useful to the world — is rooted in Carol's kitchen and her garden and what she taught him without teaching him. She is present in that room even when she isn't there.

nodes.name: Connor Huang
nodes.description: Fully embedded in the skater group. He and Drew still nod in the hallway. They're friendly without being friends. The drift completed sometime in seventh grade without a fight or a conversation — just the slow divergence of two people who shared a childhood but not an adolescence.

nodes.name: Kyle Briggs
nodes.description: Bigger now — football team, weight room. Part of the athlete faction that dominates eighth-grade social hierarchy. He and Drew have had no direct conflict since elementary school, but Kyle is part of the power structure that eighth grade is about to sharpen.

nodes.name: Marcus Webb
nodes.description: Drew's closest peer and intellectual partner. Three years of building together — from ugly HTML to a functioning message board for Tran's gym. Marcus handles hardware and networking; Drew handles software and systems architecture. Marcus has an easy social warmth that Drew observes and respects but doesn't try to replicate. They are fully honest with each other about thinking. Emotional honesty has been in the channel for a few weeks now. Thursday marked a new turn: Drew came to Marcus after lunch not to process an idea but to ask for advice — how to tell his parents about Sofia. Marcus didn't make it complicated. He cut to the practical: 'So tell your dad first.' That was the first time Marcus operated as emotional advisor, not just intellectual partner. The channel now runs in both directions across both registers.

nodes.name: Mrs. Halverson
nodes.description: Sixth-grade homeroom. Remembered fondly as the calm room. Drew occasionally sees her in the hallway and she calls him 'friend' and he means it when he smiles back.

nodes.name: Mr. Tran
nodes.description: Three years of training under Mr. Tran. The relationship has deepened from instructor-student to something closer to mentor-apprentice. Mr. Tran still communicates primarily through correction and the occasional nod, but the trust is real. He pushed Drew into competition, adjusted training when Drew joined school wrestling, and has started letting Drew help teach the younger kids' fundamentals class — a mark of respect in the gym's hierarchy. Elena Reyes mentioned her sister-in-law's nephew trains at Tran's — a potential ambient connection that Drew does not yet know about.

nodes.name: Coach Davis
nodes.description: Jasper Middle School wrestling coach. Runs practice like a drill sergeant. Recognized Drew's cross-training advantage — the hip movement from muay thai, the ground instincts from jiu-jitsu. Respects Drew's work ethic. Another authority figure who communicates through the language of effort.

nodes.name: Sofia Reyes
nodes.description: Honors track since sixth grade. Plays violin in orchestra. Quiet and deliberate — not shy, but selective; she chooses her words and her people with the same care. The courtyard lunches have been running several weeks. On Thursday she invited Drew to her concert and to meet her parents. Friday evening he came. He sat in the audience while she performed and she found him in the crowd — three seconds of eye contact before the music started. She brought him into the lobby afterward and introduced him to Elena and Luis. He held up. He was himself — the same person who wrote the letter — under the examination she knew her mother would provide. She looked back once before her family left the lobby. The test she hadn't called a test returned the answer she'd been hoping for. She is no longer only wading in.

nodes.name: Elena Reyes
nodes.description: Sofia's mother. Warm but precise — her questions are never idle; they are diagnostic without being clinical. She asked Drew about Sofia and he gave her 'perspective,' a word that landed exactly right. She asked if he heard the difference in the Barber solo and he had. When Drew described staying with difficulty because it gives you space to be uncomfortable, something useful to evolve toward, Elena said 'Útil' under her breath — the Spanish word for useful, a private confirmation of something she'd recognized. She and Luis exchanged a look. She extended the dinner invitation before the conversation was over, and it was real — not the polite gesture that functions as a social exit. She is attentive to the quality of a person's thinking, not just their credentials. Drew passed the examination she conducts through conversation. She is now actively interested in knowing him better.

nodes.name: Luis Reyes
nodes.description: Sofia's father. Quieter than Elena in social settings, but not passive — he measures through observation and then asks the question that lands on the nerve. He asked Drew about the martial arts: most kids try something and move on, so why stay? Drew answered from somewhere real: difficulty gives you space to be uncomfortable, a challenge to overcome to evolve. If it were easy, it wouldn't be worth committing to. Luis nodded: 'That's right.' It was not a performative nod. He shook Drew's hand before leaving — a firm handshake offered to a young man, not a child. He respects commitment that has been earned rather than performed. He has not yet formed a complete judgment but the first impression is strongly positive.

edges.visibility: moderate-high — Greg is present enough, and Thursday evening opened a new channel. He sees Drew more clearly now than he did a week ago.
edges.information_flow: direct and warming — Drew came to Greg about Sofia before Karen knew. Greg engaged as a near-equal, gave real advice, expressed quiet pride. The tech projects (the message board, the Alcatel interest) are now a secondary channel Greg is actively cultivating. Drew has experienced his father as an ally who covers for him. Greg will learn about the Reyes parents through Drew — probably at a natural moment, not a formal report. He will be interested in Luis specifically.

edges.visibility: high — Karen drove him to the concert. She was physically present at the threshold of an event she couldn't enter. Her awareness of what she isn't seeing has increased.
edges.information_flow: filtered and increasingly monitored — Karen drove Drew to the concert and picked him up at nine. She asked questions in the car that were not quite the questions she wanted to ask. She will want to know about the Reyes family — this is her information vector, the parental one, and it is legitimate. She will likely ask Drew about Elena and Luis, and what Drew tells her will shape her assessment of Sofia and the access Karen is willing to grant. The easy child narrative is under pressure. The monitoring is ambient for now, but this evening confirmed the pattern: Drew is in a situation she cannot fully see into, and she is learning to ask differently.

edges.visibility: low — parallel lives in the same house. But Drew is absorbing data from Megan's emotional volatility as an observer.
edges.information_flow: one-directional — Drew observes Megan's relationship drama and emotional patterns. Megan barely registers Drew's interior life.

edges.visibility: high — Saturday afternoons, unstructured, fully honest
edges.information_flow: direct, unfiltered, and now fully open — Carol will hear about the concert, about Elena and Luis, about the dinner invitation. She will hear the full version: the three seconds of eye contact before the music started, what Luis asked, what Drew said, what Elena said under her breath. Drew will not realize until he is telling her that 'Útil' lands in the same world Carol built for him — the garden, the skills, the usefulness. Carol will know what that means. She will receive it without making it larger than it is, which will make it exactly as large as it should be.

edges.visibility: low — hallway nods, occasional shared lunch, but the friendship has completed its drift
edges.information_flow: ambient — they know of each other's lives through the school grapevine, not through each other.

edges.visibility: very high — two classes, Wednesday afternoons, Saturday projects, constant text/AIM thread
edges.information_flow: direct, fully bidirectional — intellectual and emotional. Marcus will hear about the concert Monday at the latest. Drew will tell him about Elena's questions, Luis's handshake, the dinner invitation. Marcus will register the social significance of parents who invited the kid to dinner within the first conversation. He will ask the right questions, which are probably practical ones.

edges.visibility: moderate — same grade, same cafeteria, same social ecosystem. Kyle is part of the athlete power structure.
edges.information_flow: ambient — no direct communication, but eighth-grade social dynamics are smaller and more pressurized than sixth. Everyone is more visible to everyone.

edges.visibility: high — three sessions per week plus tournament travel. Mr. Tran now trusts Drew to help teach fundamentals to younger kids.
edges.information_flow: deepening task-oriented — still communicated primarily through technique, but the mentorship is real. Mr. Tran occasionally offers life advice disguised as fighting advice. Ambient note: Elena Reyes mentioned her sister-in-law's nephew trains at Tran's. This is a latent connection Drew does not yet know about. If it surfaces, Tran becomes an unexpected information bridge between Drew and the Reyes family's social world.

edges.visibility: moderate — wrestling season only, but Coach Davis remembers and respects effort year to year
edges.information_flow: institutional — Coach Davis communicates through the wrestling program. The relationship exists within that structure.

edges.visibility: high and confirmed — she found him in the crowd before the music started. Three seconds. The concert was not a neutral event; it was a shared one. The lobby afterward expanded the shared space into her family's world.
edges.information_flow: bidirectional and deepening into structure — Drew held up under the examination he knew was coming. He was himself: 'captivating,' the Barber solo, the martial arts answer, the grandmother and the garden. The thing Sofia was hoping for — that the person who wrote the letter would still be present in the lobby under her mother's questions — was confirmed. The dinner invitation her parents extended is now live on Drew's side. What he does with it will be meaningful to her.

edges.visibility: moderate — one meeting, direct and substantive. She is now a named presence in his social world, not an abstraction.
edges.information_flow: just opened and already real — Elena asked questions that Drew answered from somewhere genuine. She heard 'captivating' about her daughter's mind. She heard the grandmother and the garden. She said 'Útil' and extended the dinner invitation. The channel is new but not shallow. Drew knows she is Sofia's mother and that she is not performing warmth — she is measuring, and the measurement returned something she valued. The obligation is low but present: she invited him to dinner, and that invitation has to be honored or declined with equal genuineness.

edges.visibility: moderate — one meeting, brief but substantive on the martial arts question. Luis measured him and found something.
edges.information_flow: just opened, sparse but weighted — Luis asked the one question that mattered to him and Drew answered it from somewhere real. The handshake was offered to a young man. Drew carries Luis's nod — 'That's right' — as something that landed.

edges.visibility: high and now active — she found him in the crowd. She brought him into the lobby. She looked back once.
edges.information_flow: deliberate and now confirmed — Sofia does not open channels by accident. She opened this one, on her terms, and Friday it extended into the hardest test: her parents, in public, with the full weight of her family's assessment present. Drew passed. The gate Sofia has been holding is not fully open — that is not how she operates — but it is more open than it was Thursday, and the dinner invitation her parents extended means she will see him again in a context she trusts. The single look back before leaving the lobby was not performance. It was a private signal between two people who now share something that has a shape.

edges.visibility: high — she has been thinking about this young man since Sofia mentioned him. The lobby confirmed the mental image and exceeded it in several specific ways.
edges.information_flow: evaluative and now positive — Elena heard 'captivating' about Sofia's mind from a fourteen-year-old boy and recognized it as accurate rather than flattering. She heard him connect the Barber solo to discipline and structure, and he had. She heard him articulate why he stays with difficulty — the philosophical answer, not the martial arts answer — and she said 'Útil' to herself. She and Luis exchanged a look. She extended the dinner invitation before the conversation was over. Her conclusion is positive and is not provisional in the way it was before the concert. She will tell Luis tonight what she heard and what she thinks. The dinner invitation will be real when it is extended again.

gatekeepers.gates: social access — who Drew can visit, where he can go, what activities he's allowed to do, how late he can stay out
gatekeepers.conditions: academic performance, good behavior, church attendance, being low-maintenance
gatekeepers.current_stance: permissive but actively recalibrating — Karen drove Drew to the concert and picked him up at nine. She managed the logistics, as she always does. But the questions in the car were the questions of a mother who knows there is a room she cannot enter. She will want to know about the Reyes family — this is legitimate parental inquiry, and she will frame it that way. If Drew tells her about Elena and Luis in a way that reassures her (which the facts support: dinner invitation, warm reception, good family), Karen's monitoring is likely to stabilize rather than escalate. If he closes the channel, the monitoring tightens. The concert itself was an approved event; the dinner invitation is the next threshold she will need to approve.

gatekeepers.gates: autonomy and responsibility — what tasks Drew is trusted with, how much independence he gets in practical matters
gatekeepers.conditions: demonstrated competence, following through on tasks without being reminded
gatekeepers.current_stance: wide open and newly engaged — Greg treats Drew as functionally reliable, and Thursday's conversation moved the relationship into new territory. Greg is not just a passive gatekeeper of competence metrics now; he is actively interested in Drew's life and in maintaining the channel while it's open. He knows the window is finite. The Alcatel/tech bridge is a secondary structure he's building to sustain contact. Trust is at its highest point and the connection is more personal than it has been.

gatekeepers.gates: access to reciprocal connection — whether this becomes a real relationship, what kind of closeness is possible, at what pace
gatekeepers.conditions: genuine interest without performance, respect for her pace and deliberateness, substance over social maneuvering, consistency between what he wrote and who he actually is
gatekeepers.current_stance: open and advancing — Friday resolved the most pressurized test the gate has presented so far. Drew came into her family's world and remained himself. The gate's extension into family space is complete; what follows is whether Drew uses it. The dinner invitation from Elena and Luis means the next step exists. Sofia will not push it — she doesn't operate that way — but she is watching whether he follows through.

gatekeepers.gates: access to the Reyes family space — dinner, home visits, continued contact with Sofia in family contexts. Elena's assessment shapes how much room Sofia has to develop this connection within her family's world. A positive assessment from Elena signals to Sofia that this relationship is safe to invest in; a negative one creates friction even if it doesn't prohibit.
gatekeepers.conditions: genuine character, intellectual seriousness, respect for Sofia as a person rather than a social prize, usefulness as a value (not performance of usefulness), the capacity to be present without performing
gatekeepers.current_stance: open — the dinner invitation was extended within the first meeting. Elena does not extend this invitation to people who have not passed her examination. The conditions were met: 'captivating,' the Barber solo, the martial arts answer, the grandmother and the garden, 'Útil.' Her gate is currently permissive, and the dinner itself will be the next evaluation. The standard will be the same: is he real, and is he good for her daughter?

rewarded.reward: trust, expanded autonomy, pride currency from Greg, relief from Karen's worry
delegation of responsibility (Greg), being left alone to think (Carol), not triggering anxiety (Karen)
tournament results, physical confidence, respect from the gym and the wrestling team. This now partially satisfies the sports-participation norm that Drew previously sidestepped.
social invisibility — the kind that means adults don't worry about you

ACTIVE AND ESCALATING — parental approval from the Reyes family is now active currency. Elena and Luis are not neutral parties: they are adults who assessed Drew directly and found him worth inviting to dinner. This is a new and significant form of social validation that operates outside the peer ecosystem. Within the peer ecosystem, the honors track is a small world; a concert attendance and parental introduction from Sofia Reyes will travel. Drew's position in the eighth-grade social order continues to shift, slowly and in his favor.

CONFIRMED AND EXPANDING — Drew told Marcus, told Carol, told Greg. Each disclosure returned something: Marcus gave practical counsel, Carol gave insight and confirmation, Greg gave pride and alliance. Three data points now. The pattern is established: choosing the right person and opening the channel does not cost you. It deepens the relationship and returns something real.

EMERGING — Thursday was the first time Drew routed something significant through Greg rather than around him. Greg covered for Drew with Karen without being asked. The reward was discovering that Greg is an active ally, not just a passive one. This dynamic will encourage Drew to use this channel again when something is complicated.

NEWLY ACTIVE — the concert lobby produced a new reward structure. Drew did not perform for Elena and Luis; he answered from somewhere real, and the real answer was sufficient. The reward is the dinner invitation, which is also access to Sofia in family space. More broadly, Drew now has evidence that being himself under pressure from adults he doesn't know — adults who matter — returns something. The philosophical answer Luis heard about difficulty and commitment was the same answer Drew would have given Marcus. That consistency was the thing Elena and Luis were measuring for.

punished.cost: anxiety spike in Karen, uncomfortable silence from Greg, social embarrassment. Drew has internalized this so thoroughly that punishment is rarely needed — he self-polices.
loss of autonomy, increased monitoring, Karen's worry shifting from background to foreground
loss of trust, which for Drew is loss of the currency he values most
ACTIVE — eighth grade's hardening social factions are beginning to punish the space between groups. Drew's drifter strategy may start carrying a cost it didn't carry before.

punished.behavior: performing rather than being genuine with Sofia
punished.cost: ACTIVE AND CONFIRMED AT HIGHER STAKES — the concert lobby raised the floor. Drew was genuine under Elena's examination and the examination returned confirmation. Sofia knows what genuine looks like from him now; Elena knows what it looks like. If Drew begins to manage his presentation — performing the version of himself he thinks will satisfy the Reyes family at dinner — both of them will read it. The cost is not just loss of access to the family. It is loss of the specific thing Sofia was responding to, and loss of the credibility Elena just granted.

punished.behavior: romantic rejection — public or widely known
punished.cost: REDUCED but not eliminated — the letter reached Sofia without public exposure, and her response was private and positive. The concert attendance and parental introduction are becoming known in the honors track's small ecosystem. The acute risk of humiliation has passed. The ongoing risk is that what happens between Drew and Sofia will eventually be visible to that ecosystem.

ACTIVE — Karen drove Drew to the concert and asked questions that weren't the questions she wanted to ask. She is learning to ask differently because the direct questions haven't worked. What Drew tells her about the Reyes family — Elena's warmth, the dinner invitation, who Luis is — will determine whether her monitoring stabilizes or escalates. The cost of continued filtering is incremental: Karen is not paranoid, but she is competent, and she will construct a picture from what she can observe. Better for Drew if she constructs it from what he tells her.

punished.behavior: failing to follow through on the dinner invitation
punished.cost: NEWLY ACTIVE — Elena's dinner invitation was real. Failing to follow through — through avoidance, awkwardness, or not bringing it up with Karen — signals the opposite of what the lobby conversation communicated. The invitation was extended as a result of genuine engagement; letting it lapse without acknowledgment would be its own statement. Sofia will notice.
